data_quality: v0.1 best-effort seed populated from cited public sources. Each entity's specs trace to source_ids on its attributes. Verification pass required before production launch; this dataset is sized to prove the engine end-to-end, not for general consumer trust.

sources.name: Manufacturer product pages (Boss, Strymon, EHX, Walrus Audio, JHS Pedals, Chase Bliss Audio, Pedaltrain, CIOKS, Truetone, Voodoo Lab, MXR, TC Electronic, Korg, Peterson, Cusack Music)
sources.scope: Used for current draw (mA), voltage, polarity, true-bypass status, dimensions, weight, output counts, and isolated-output specs. Each product page is the canonical source for electrical specs.

sources.name: Reverb.com public listings and community threads
sources.scope: Used to cross-check real-world compatibility reports, user-reported current draw under load, and pricing benchmarks against manufacturer MSRP.

sources.name: The Gear Page community forums (public threads)
sources.scope: Used for practical pedalboard layout caveats, power supply noise reports with specific pedal pairings, and community-maintained spec corrections for legacy pedals (RAT, Big Muff variants, CE-2W).

sources.name: Pedalboard-planner blog tutorials and layout guides (PedalboardPlanner.com, SweetWater Pedalboard Guide, Andertons Pedalboard Wiring articles)
sources.scope: Used for signal-chain ordering conventions, patch-cable count formulas, power daisy-chain vs isolated output recommendations, and pedalboard real-estate sizing heuristics.

sources.name: CIOKS, Truetone, and Voodoo Lab public spec sheets and FAQ pages
sources.scope: Used for output voltage options, total current budget, isolated vs non-isolated output maps, and daisy-chain current-share warnings on specific units.

sources.name: TalkBass and GuitarSite community threads (public)
sources.scope: Used to capture bass-rig pedalboard compatibility edge cases, particularly for fuzz pedals sensitive to input impedance and for Big Muff variants on low-tuned instruments.

attributes.note: Buffered bypass. Classic mid-hump overdrive; JRC4558D chip in original. High sensitivity to input impedance — place before wahs and buffers. Daisy-chain safe (analog, low draw).
True bypass, JFET input. HP/LP switch changes clipping voicing. Very low draw — compatible with any output including daisy-chain.
True bypass. Internal charge pump doubles supply to 18V internally — do NOT feed 18V externally. Analog, low draw, daisy-chain safe.
Digital control plane over analog audio. High draw (~165mA) — requires isolated output; do not daisy-chain. MIDI over TRS-A.
Buffered bypass. LM308 op-amp in legacy units. Extremely low draw — any output or daisy-chain suitable. Op-amp rolloff produces distinctive filter characteristic.
True bypass reissue. Triangle/Russian variants differ by clipping diodes. Input impedance ~470kΩ; place after buffers if using passive pickups. Daisy-chain safe.
Buffered bypass (Boss standard). Low draw, ACA vs PSA supply generations differ in regulated/unregulated behavior — modern units use PSA (center-negative 9V). Daisy-chain safe.
Op-amp clipping variant of Big Muff; more mid-forward than triangle/Russian. Daisy-chain safe. Low draw.
Six Big Muff variants on a rotary switch. Slightly higher draw than single-mode Muff units due to switching circuitry. Isolated output recommended but daisy-chain tolerable.
Fuzz + tremolo in one enclosure. Joystick control. Higher draw — use isolated output to avoid crosstalk with other pedals. Digital LFO section is noise-sensitive.

Waza Craft analog BBD chorus with CE-1 mode. Buffered bypass. 100mA draw higher than typical Boss pedals due to dual BBD path. Isolated output strongly recommended — BBD clock noise can couple on shared rails.
Script or block logo variants differ in op-amp type. Buffered bypass. Ultra-low draw — any output or daisy-chain fine.
DSP modulation multi-effect. 300mA draw — MUST use isolated output; will reboot or audibly glitch on shared rails. 9V only (no 12V/18V). MIDI over DIN-5.
DSP delay multi-effect. 300mA isolated output mandatory. MIDI DIN-5 + USB. Place at or near end of chain. Do not share rail with any other pedal.
Analog BBD delay (MN3005 or equivalent). Very low draw; daisy-chain safe. Buffered bypass. BBD clock at low frequencies can produce subtle noise — isolated output preferred for quiet rigs.
Digital delay; 11 modes. Buffered bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended but not mandatory if supply has headroom. USB for computer control.

DSP reverb multi-effect. 300mA isolated output mandatory. MIDI DIN-5. Place last or second-to-last in chain. Same platform as TimeLine / Mobius — Strymon Ojai/Zuma power supplies have purpose-built high-current isolated outputs for these.

entities.name: Walrus Audio Slö Multi Texture Reverb

attributes.note: DSP reverb. 100mA — isolated output recommended. Sub, Rise, and Sustain modes optimized for ambient/post-rock applications. DECAY knob doubles as infinite reverb toggle at max.
Entry-level touring board; fits 4–5 standard-size pedals. Underneath rails accommodate most compact power supplies (Voodoo Lab PP2+, Truetone 1 SPOT Pro CS6). Soft case included.
Mid-size touring board with two riser levels. Fits 8–10 standard pedals. Undercarriage accommodates most full-size power supplies. Soft case included; hard case upgrade available.
Full touring board; fits 12–16 pedals across three riser levels. Tour-grade aluminum rails. TSA-lockable hard case sold separately. Standard choice for professional live rigs.
Updated rail profile with wider spacing for larger enclosures. Undercarriage deeper than Classic series — fits Strymon Zuma and CIOKS DC10 without modification. Soft case included.
Flat-profile metro-series board. Single riser level; best for players who want a lower profile on stage. Slightly thinner undercarriage — confirm power supply depth before ordering.
Ultra-compact fly-gig board; fits 3–4 compact pedals. Backpack-compatible soft case. Power supply must be a mini format (1 SPOT, PP Nano+) — full-size units will not fit underneath.

Industry-standard isolated supply. Outputs 1–4: 100mA each at 9V. Outputs 5–6: 250mA each (switchable 9V/12V). Outputs 7–8: 120mA each (switchable 9V/18V). Sag control on outputs 5–6 for vintage fuzz voicing.

10 fully isolated outputs with flex-linking for high-current digital pedals (Strymon trio: two outputs linked = 600mA). Toroidal transformer for near-zero magnetic hum. Best-in-class for noise-sensitive rigs.

12 isolated outputs. Outputs 1–8: 100mA at 9V. Output 9: 250mA (9/12V). Outputs 10–11: 250mA at 18V. Output 12: 400mA at 9V. Flat profile fits Pedaltrain Classic and Novo undercarriage without modification.
9 isolated outputs; 5 at 300mA and 4 at 100mA (9V). Designed specifically for Strymon pedal trio — one 300mA output per BigSky/TimeLine/Mobius with no linking required. Linear regulated.

Non-isolated supply; outputs share common ground. Fine for all-analog boards where pedal isolation is not needed. Outputs 1–8: 100mA at 9V (two share a daisy-chain capable bus). Outputs 9–10: 250mA at 18V. Avoid pairing with digital pedals on same rail.

10 daisy-chained outputs at 9.6V (slightly above 9V nominal). Non-isolated — all outputs share one ground bus. Good budget option for all-analog boards. Not suitable for DSP pedals (noise, ground loops). Included banana-jack adapter for EHX pedals.
Bulk cable + solderless right-angle plugs; cut to any length. .155 diameter. Highly regarded for low capacitance and minimal high-frequency rolloff. Plugs screw-crimp — no soldering iron needed.
Soldered Neutrik right-angle TS. Quad-conductor shield for lowest noise floor. Studio-grade; heavy for dense boards but provides excellent RF rejection. Available in fixed 0.5/1/2/3/6ft lengths.

entities.name: EBS PCF Deluxe Flat Patch Cable

attributes.note: Ultra-flat ribbon profile; fits tight-spaced boards where standard right-angle plugs foul adjacent inputs. Nickel-plated plugs. Fixed 10cm/18cm/28cm sizes. Popular for pedalboard competition builds.
10 right-angle plugs + bulk cable; cut-to-length solderless. Designed to fit Pedaltrain board rail widths. Lower-cost entry to custom-length cables. Signal quality sufficient for stage use.
Solderless kit with ultra-thin cable (.095" diameter) and right-angle mini plugs. Ideal for boards where pedal spacing is tight and standard plugs don't fit. Plugs use screw-lock mechanism.

Straight TS molded plugs. Budget tier; acceptable for home practice boards. Not recommended for live rigs where cable tension on output jacks is a reliability concern. Buy solderless or right-angle for gigging boards.
Buffered bypass (mutes output on tuning, passes buffer when bypassed). Can power downstream pedals via daisy-chain output. Standard tuner for professional live rigs. ±1 cent accuracy.
True bypass strobe tuner. ±0.1 cent accuracy — highest accuracy class for intonation work. Sweetened tunings library built in. Isolated output recommended (DSP backlight draws modest current).

True bypass with built-in buffer mode (switchable). Polyphonic strumming for fast between-song checks. Buffer mode provides clean signal to downstream pedals when bypassed. Isolated output recommended.
Rackmount-style floor tuner. True bypass. High-visibility display for dark stages. Low draw. 1U rack-friendly — popular for players who want a tuner off the pedalboard. ±0.1 cent accuracy.
Dual-channel overdrive with 6 selectable circuit types per side. High draw (~165mA) — isolated output required. True bypass with relay switching. MIDI over TRS.
Low-gain Marshall-style overdrive. Extremely low draw — any output or daisy-chain safe. HP switch adds top-end clarity. True bypass.
Asymmetric clipping overdrive; warmer than the TS9. Buffered bypass. Very low draw — daisy-chain safe. Popular as a clean boost or TS alternative.
Tube Screamer-inspired with three selectable clipping modes (op-amp, diode, no clipping). True bypass. Low draw — daisy-chain safe.
High-gain distortion with 3-band EQ and mid-shift toggle. True bypass. Low draw — daisy-chain safe.
RAT2 circuit with LED clipping diodes for higher headroom and tighter low-end. Buffered bypass. Extremely low draw — any supply or daisy-chain suitable.
Germanium transistor fuzz in compact enclosure. Very input-impedance sensitive — place first in chain or after a buffer-bypass pedal for consistent tone. Ultra-low draw; daisy-chain safe.
Five selectable fuzz circuits on rotary. True bypass. Moderate draw — daisy-chain tolerable but isolated preferred.
4-stage analog phaser. Buffered bypass. Ultra-low draw — daisy-chain safe. Color switch alters feedback intensity.
Stereo chorus with separate EQ knob. Buffered bypass. Low draw — daisy-chain safe. Stereo out for wet/dry rig configurations.
Solid-state talk box; speaker-to-tube driver eliminates external amp requirement. Buffered bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended.
Reissue of the original analog DM-2 using MN3205 BBD. Waza mode extends delay time. Buffered bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended to prevent BBD clock noise coupling.
Analog BBD delay with chorus/vibrato modulation on repeats. True bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output preferred. Expression pedal input for delay-time sweeps.
Digital delay with tap tempo and lo-fi switch. True bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended. USB for firmware updates.
Compact digital delay with three modes (digital, analog, tape). True bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended for quiet rigs.
Spring, Hall, Flerb, and Plate reverbs. Buffered bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended. Classic budget reverb pedal for all styles.
8 reverb types including Grail Spring, Hall, Plate, Reverse, and Echo Room. Stereo in/out. Buffered bypass. 100mA — isolated output recommended. Expression pedal input for reverb time.
Budget-tier digital reverb; hall, spring, shimmer modes. True bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended.
OTA-based compressor with blend knob for parallel compression. True bypass. Moderate draw — daisy-chain tolerable but isolated preferred for low-noise operation.
Dynacomp-style OTA compressor with clean blend and preamp boost. True bypass. Low draw — daisy-chain safe.
1176-style FET compressor in compact enclosure. True bypass. Higher draw (130mA) — isolated output recommended. Attack/release controls plus wet/dry blend.
Simple two-knob OTA compressor. Buffered bypass. Ultra-low draw — daisy-chain safe. Script logo vintage units slightly different circuit. Classic country/funk squish.
Classic inductor-based wah. Buffered bypass with click-on/off footswitch. Very low draw — daisy-chain safe. Halo inductor is the signature component; aftermarket Fasel inductors popular upgrade.
Budget reissue of the classic Vox wah circuit. Buffered bypass. Ultra-low draw — daisy-chain safe. Italian-made Fasel inductor variant available as upgrade.
Compact flat-profile metro board; fits 3–4 standard pedals. Soft case included. Undercarriage fits Strymon Ojai and PP Nano+ — confirm depth before ordering larger supplies.
Micro board for 2–3 compact pedals or fly-gig essentials. Soft bag included. Power supply must be a 1 SPOT or similar flat-bottom unit — no undercarriage depth for full-size supplies.
Flagship Novo-series board; wide rail spacing accommodates large enclosures (Strymon BigSky, CIOKS DC10). Three riser levels. Soft case included; hard case upgrade available.
Canvas-covered steel-rail board with integrated Mono case. Meets carry-on airline size requirements (medium variant). Rugged for touring; slightly heavier than aluminum options.

High-end welded aluminum board with dual-level rail system and integrated cable management channels. No case included — sold separately. Popular among pro session and touring guitarists for clean build aesthetics.
Boutique welded-steel board with anodized finish. Slanted rail profile. No case included. Custom orders available. Popular with players who prefer a heavier/sturdier option over aluminum.
7 isolated outputs with user-selectable voltages (9/12/18V per output via internal jumpers). Compact chassis. Suitable for boards with mixed-voltage pedals. Outputs 1–5: 100mA; output 6–7: 250mA.
Ultra-high-current isolated supply (500mA per output). Designed for DSP-heavy rigs with multiple Strymon/Eventide/Chase Bliss pedals. Linear regulated. Expandable via GigRig Quartermaster.
Expander module for GigRig Generator; adds 4 additional isolated 9V outputs at 250mA each. Powered from Generator's expansion port — requires Generator host. Not a standalone supply.

5 isolated 9V outputs at 300mA each. Linear regulated for ultra-low noise. Purpose-built for high-current digital pedals; each output can independently handle a Strymon BigSky/TimeLine/Mobius. Expandable with Ojai R30 Expansion Kit.

Ultra-compact isolated supply; 5 outputs at 150mA each (9V). Designed to mount beneath pedalboards with minimal footprint. Good option for medium-draw digital boards. Not suitable for 300mA Strymon pedals without output combining.

Budget-tier isolated supply. 6 outputs at 100mA (9V), 1 output at 12V (100mA), 1 output at 18V (100mA). Switching supply — noise floor higher than linear units. Acceptable for all-analog or low-sensitivity digital boards.
Ultra-thin .095" cable with screw-lock right-angle mini plugs; cut to any length. Ideal for tight-spaced boards. Separate from the standard Lava Tightrope entry — this is the 10-plug bundle variant.

entities.name: Voodoo Lab Pedal Power Solder-Free Patch Cable Kit

attributes.note: 10 right-angle screw-crimp plugs + bulk cable. Voodoo Lab brand; compatible with any standard TRS/TS jack. Low-capacitance cable. Often bundled with Pedal Power 2 Plus packages.
Solderless right-angle patch system with color-coded plugs for signal chain identification. 10-plug kit. Used by touring professionals. Cable is OFC for low resistance over long patch runs.
Flat-profile solderless variant; 10 plugs + bulk cable. Combines the space-saving flat profile of EBS PCF Deluxe with the flexibility of cut-to-length assembly.
Pack of 5 right-angle molded patch cables (4"/10cm). Budget tier; acceptable for small boards. Not recommended for dense pro rigs where cable quality affects noise floor.
Pack of 3 straight TS molded patch cables (3"/7.5cm). Lowest-cost option. Straight plugs can create mechanical stress on pedal jacks in tight builds — use only for home practice or temporary rigs.
True bypass chromatic tuner with full-color display and multiple meter modes. ±0.5 cent accuracy. Low profile; fits compact boards. Low draw — daisy-chain safe.
True bypass strobe tuner with ±0.02 cent accuracy — fastest-tracking and most accurate class available in pedal format. Low draw — daisy-chain safe. Popular for intonation work and alternate tunings.
Multistage JFET/BJT overdrive with three gain structure modes (low, mid, high). High draw (165mA) — isolated output required. True bypass with relay switching. MIDI over TRS-A.
Dual-channel overdrive with 6 selectable circuit types per side. High draw (165mA) — isolated output required. True bypass with relay switching. MIDI over TRS-A.
DSP tape-echo emulator with three tape-type modes and tape-age/crinkle controls. 250mA — isolated output mandatory. MIDI over TRS-A. Place after overdrives, before reverb.
Amp and IR cab simulator for direct recording or FOH. Three amp models (round, chime, punch) with selectable IR cabs. 300mA isolated output mandatory. Stereo out. USB for IR loading.
Klon-inspired mini overdrive. True bypass. Ultra-compact enclosure. Very low draw — daisy-chain safe. Internal charge pump generates higher internal voltage headroom.
Low-gain transparent overdrive with treble and bass shelving. True bypass. Low draw — daisy-chain safe. Popular as a clean boost or always-on tone shaper.
9 Tube Screamer variants on a rotary switch (TS-808, TS9, TS10, OD-1, etc.). True bypass. Moderate draw — daisy-chain tolerable but isolated preferred due to switching circuitry.
Low-gain Marshall-style overdrive. True bypass. HP switch adds top-end clarity. Extremely low draw — any output or daisy-chain safe.
Four delay modes (digital, analog, lo-fi, swell) with tap tempo. True bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended. Compact enclosure for a feature-rich digital delay.
12 reverb algorithms with two independent engines for dual-reverb or stereo effects. Buffered bypass. High draw (230mA) — isolated output required. Expression pedal input.
Iconic analog BBD delay with chorus/vibrato modulation on repeats. Buffered bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output preferred to prevent BBD clock noise coupling. Stereo output.
Asymmetric JFET-clipping overdrive with wide gain range into near-distortion. Buffered bypass. Very low draw — daisy-chain safe. Popular mod target (Keeley, Monte Allums).
OTA-based compressor with tone and attack controls. Buffered bypass. Very low draw — daisy-chain safe. Popular mod target for reduced noise floor (Keeley CS-3 mod).
Bass-optimized overdrive with MDP (Multi-Dimensional Processing) technology; preserves low-end clarity during gain. Buffered bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended.

entities.name: MXR M169 Carbon Copy Analog Delay

attributes.note: Analog BBD delay up to 600ms with modulation switch. Buffered bypass. Very low draw — daisy-chain safe. Modulation adds chorus-like warble to repeats.
Mini enclosure with both Phase 45 and Phase 90 circuits selectable, plus script/block toggle per mode. Buffered bypass. Ultra-low draw — daisy-chain safe.
Stereo reverse delay + reverb with expression control over most parameters. True bypass with relay switching. High draw (130mA) — isolated output recommended. Tap tempo.
Advanced modulated delay with three delay modes and onboard reverb. True bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended. Extensive modulation controls for ambient textures.
Five reverb modes with self-oscillation and decay-into-chaos capability. True bypass. Moderate draw — isolated output recommended. Designed for experimental and noise applications.

entities.name: Old Blood Noise Endeavors Black Fountain

attributes.note: Oil-can delay emulation with three repeat voicings (oil can, tape, wash). True bypass. Moderate draw — daisy-chain tolerable, isolated preferred. Tap tempo.
Canvas-covered steel-rail board with integrated Mono case. 24" width accommodates medium rigs. Meets airline carry-on size in most cases. Durable touring-grade construction.
Slanted aluminum rail board with soft gig bag. Low-profile single riser. Undercarriage accommodates compact power supplies. Good value mid-size option.
Flat-bottom board with hook-and-loop top surface for velcro-mount pedals (no rail slots). Full-size 32" deck. Soft case included. Popular for players who prefer velcro over zip ties.
Extra-large flat-surface velcro-mount board. Hook-and-loop top. 42" length for large professional rigs. Soft case included. Hard case upgrade available.
Flat perforated aluminum board; pedals mount via bolt-through hardware or zip-tie through holes. No riser. Ultra-lightweight — suitable for fly gigs. No case included. Compatible with 1/4" T-bolts.
Aluminum-rail board with integrated hardshell case. Two riser levels. Undercarriage designed to fit T-Rex Fuel Tank supplies. TSA-lockable latches.
Mono's stealth-series board with black anodized finish and reinforced EVA case. Single riser. Integrated cable management along rear rail. Carry-on compatible.
Boutique aluminum board with proprietary clip-mount system (no velcro, no zip ties — pedals click onto rail). Two riser levels. Hardshell case included. Quick-change pedal swaps without tools.
5 isolated outputs: 4 at 100mA (9V) and 1 at 250mA switchable 9V/12V. Compact chassis fits Pedaltrain undercarriage. Good entry-level isolated supply for small-to-medium analog boards.
8 fully isolated outputs with flex-linking for high-current pedals (two linked outputs = up to 500mA combined). Toroidal transformer. Fits Pedaltrain Novo undercarriage. Compact sibling to the DC10.
6 isolated outputs; 4 at 100mA (9V), 2 at 300mA (9V/12V). Flat profile — fits compact Pedaltrain Metro/Nano undercarriage. USB-A charging port included. Good mid-size isolated option.

5 isolated 9V outputs at 200mA each. Linear regulated. Compact and flat — fits Metro and Voyager undercarriage. Expandable via Ojai Expansion Kit. Not suitable for 300mA Strymon pedals without output linking.
8 isolated outputs; 4 at 100mA and 4 at 400mA (9V/12V/18V selectable per section). High-current outputs handle two Strymon-class DSP pedals simultaneously without linking. Rack-mount or under-board.

Swiss-made low-profile right-angle plugs with square barrel that sits flush against pedal face. Bulk cable + screw-crimp plugs. Extremely compact — ideal for boards where plug height clears adjacent knobs.

entities.name: EBS PCF DLX Flat Patch Set

attributes.note: Factory-terminated flat-ribbon patch cables. Nickel-plated right-angle TS plugs. Available in 10cm, 18cm, and 28cm fixed lengths. Ultra-flat profile fits tight-spaced boards; popular for competition builds.
Solderless kit with ELC (Extra Low Capacitance) cable and miniature right-angle plugs. Preserves high-frequency content better than standard cable in long patch runs. 10-plug bundle.
Ultra-thin .095" cable with screw-lock mini right-angle plugs. Cut to any length. Ideal for boards where standard right-angle plugs physically foul knobs or switches on adjacent pedals.

Studio-grade spiral-shield bulk cable sold by the foot; requires soldering to plugs. W2319 uses neglex spiral shielding for extremely low capacitance and high RF rejection. Industry standard for custom patch builds.
Barrel-to-USB-A power cable for pedals with USB power input. Not an audio patch cable — used to route power from USB-equipped power supplies (Cioks Crux USB port) to pedals accepting USB power.
Hand-soldered TRS (stereo) right-angle patch cable; used for MIDI-over-TRS connections between MIDI-capable pedals (Strymon, Chase Bliss, Disaster Area controllers). Neutrik plugs.
24-plug solderless right-angle kit with color-coded plugs for signal-chain identification. OFC cable for low resistance. Large-format kit for full professional builds; used by touring engineers.
5-pack of 10cm right-angle molded patch cables. Budget tier. Suitable for compact practice boards. Not recommended for dense pro builds where cable quality affects noise floor.
Single molded straight TS patch cable (3"/7.5cm). Entry-level. Straight plugs create mechanical stress on output jacks in tight builds — use right-angle alternatives for gigging boards.

entities.name: Boss TU-3W Waza Craft Chromatic Tuner

attributes.note: Waza Craft edition of TU-3 with true bypass mode (switchable to buffered). Higher-contrast display and improved tracking vs standard TU-3. Can daisy-chain power to downstream pedals in buffer mode.
True bypass chromatic pedal tuner with four display modes (Regular, Strobe, Half-Strobe, Mirror). ±0.1 cent accuracy. Low draw — daisy-chain safe.
Compact mini pedal tuner with true bypass. ±0.1 cent accuracy. Very low draw and small footprint — ideal for fly-gig boards or as a secondary tuner. Bright LED display.
Clip-on chromatic tuner; battery powered, no pedalboard power draw. ±0.5 cent accuracy. Not in signal chain — used as a backup or for quiet stage tuning without muting signal.

entities.name: Snark ST-2 All Instrument Clip-On Tuner

attributes.note: Budget clip-on chromatic tuner; battery powered. Not in signal chain. ±1 cent accuracy. Popular as a practice-room backup. Rotating display for various instrument orientations.
Clip-on strobe tuner with ±0.1 cent accuracy. Battery powered; zero pedalboard power draw. Sweetened tunings library. HD display for daylight readability. Best clip-on accuracy available.

Compact version of PolyTune 3 with polyphonic strumming detection and built-in buffer (switchable). True bypass when buffer disabled. Smaller footprint than full-size PolyTune 3. Isolated output recommended.
True bypass chromatic pedal tuner with full-color display and multiple meter modes. ±0.5 cent accuracy. Low profile compact footprint. Low draw — daisy-chain safe.

entities.name: Sonic Research Turbo Tuner ST-300 Mini

attributes.note: Mini-format strobe pedal tuner with ±0.02 cent accuracy. True bypass. Fast-tracking LED strobe display. Low draw — daisy-chain safe. Preferred by luthiers and intonation-focused players.

constraints.description: power_supply.total_current_ma must exceed the sum of current_draw_ma across all pedals in the configured rig. A 15% headroom margin is recommended (multiply pedal sum by 1.15 before comparing).
Each pedal's voltage_v must be available from at least one output on the selected power_supply. Pedals requiring 9V cannot use 12V/18V outputs without a voltage reducer.

Pedals of subtype delay or reverb with DSP (Chase Bliss Brothers, Strymon Mobius, Strymon TimeLine, Strymon BigSky, Walrus Slö, Boss DD-8, TC Electronic PolyTune 3) require isolated power outputs to prevent clock noise, ground loops, and digital hash coupling to analog pedals on the same rail. Non-isolated supplies (MXR DC Brick, EHX 9.6DC-200) must not be used with these pedals.

Minimum patch cables needed = number of pedals in signal chain minus 1. A tuner at the front adds one more cable from guitar to tuner input when the tuner occupies a signal-chain slot rather than a parallel out.

Sum of pedal footprint areas (dimensions_mm width × depth, converted to square inches) must not exceed 85% of the usable deck area of the selected pedalboard (board width_inch × depth_inch × 0.85). Pedal arrangement may be optimized to fit — this constraint is a first-pass filter only.
